rationale.R1.1: Section advances a named method (CalibRL) with an explicit gap statement (RL not yet used for LLM calibration) and a contribution claim; reviewing requires verifying that the framing accurately reconstructs the problem and positions the work coherently.
rationale.R1.2: The section chains several inferential steps: Brier-score reward → ECE reduction → cross-domain transfer → reward-shape ablation → deployment readiness. Logical consistency across these steps is central to a general section review.
rationale.R1.3: Multiple specific quantitative claims are asserted (ECE 0.18→0.06 on TriviaQA, 0.21→0.09 on MMLU, <0.4% accuracy drop, ECE<0.10 OOD, 1.7% log-loss accuracy drop). Verifying that each claim is grounded in the reported experiments and not overstated is load-bearing.
rationale.R1.4: The section contains an explicit universal novelty claim ('to our knowledge, no prior work has used RL directly to optimize for calibration in the LLM setting'). Assessing whether this claim survives scrutiny against the literature is load-bearing.
rationale.R1.6: A generic section review is expected to cover prose clarity and paragraph-level quality; not load-bearing but clearly within scope of 'review this section.'
rationale.R1.7: Five citation clusters are invoked to anchor the problem framing and novelty claim; checking reference accuracy and whether key relevant work is missing is central to validating the scholarship.
